review_notes: The functions provided are generally well-aligned with the NIST 800-171 control 3.1.2, focusing on limiting access to authorized users. The RBAC-related functions are particularly relevant as they directly manage user permissions. The NetworkPolicy function is also applicable as it restricts network access, which is a form of limiting system access. Naming conventions are followed correctly, and the functions are descriptive and specific.
title: 3_1_2: 3.1.2 Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute
step1_reasoning: The control 3.1.2 focuses on limiting system access to authorized users, which maps directly to Kubernetes RBAC for managing permissions and access control. RBAC ensures that users and service accounts have only the necessary permissions. Additionally, NetworkPolicies can be used to restrict network access, ensuring that only authorized ingress traffic is allowed, further limiting access to the Kubernetes environment.
